LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT KARIN THURM'S CODE CIV. PROC. SECTION 425.16 SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT, BAD FAITH, INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH BUSINESS RELATIONS, ABUSE OF PROCESS, UNFAIR COMPETITON, AND NEGLIGENCE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SATISFIED HER BURDEN THAT THE COMPLAINT IS BASED ON PROTECTED CONDUCT, WHICH IS HER FILING OF THE PRIOR LAWSUIT. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT MEET THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING ANY MERIT TO THEIR CLAIMS. THE COMPLAINT IS, IN LEGAL EFFECT, A MALICIOUS PROSECUTION SUIT, BUT LACKS THE REQUIRED ELEMENTS OF SUCH A CLAIM. DEFENDANT AWARDED $2910 (REASONABLE FEES OF $2500 PLUS COSTS OF $410). ORDER & JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT KARIN THURM'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OF WEST GROUP REAL ESTATE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION; JOHN MONTGOMERY, MITCHELL R. PETERS, LINDA V. KOCEK PETERS, RICHARD PETERS, MADELINE PETERS, DANIEL B. PETERS, ELIZABETH HOWARD PETERS.

APPEARANCES: Ross McLauren Madden, Esq. 567-1212 for deft & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Off calendar due to ruling on motion to strike. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT KARIN THURM'S CODE CIV. PROC. SECTION 425.16 SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF WRITTEN CONTRACT, BAD FAITH, INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION, INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE WITH BUSINESS RELATIONS, ABUSE OF PROCESS, UNFAIR COMPETITON, AND NEGLIGENCE.

APPEARANCES: Ross McLauren Madden, Esq. 567-1212 for deft & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted. Defendant satisfied her burden that the complaint is based on protected conduct, which is her filing of the prior lawsuit. Plaintiffs did not meet their burden of showing any merit to their claims. The complaint is, in legal effect, a malicious prosecution suit, but lacks the required elements of such a claim. Defendant awarded $2910 (reasonable fees of $2500 plus costs of $410). Order & judgment signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
